description: A local-first paper knowledge engine organized by research idea spaces, with PDF parsing, source-grounded literature search, knowledge cards, and a local MCP server for external agents.

userStories.title: Bootstrap local application foundation
userStories.description: As a personal researcher, I want to start a local application so that I can manage a paper knowledge base in my browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a local application structure with a FastAPI service, a basic Web UI entry point, and project run instructions
Provide a health check endpoint that returns service status
Provide a development command that starts the app locally
A basic test command can run successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create SQLite storage foundation
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need a stable local database schema so that spaces, papers, passages, cards, and index state can be persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize a SQLite database file and local application data directory
Create tables for spaces, papers, passages, knowledge_cards, notes, and app_state
Store space_id on every space-scoped table so future queries can isolate data by space
Database initialization can be run repeatedly without destroying existing data
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Manage idea spaces through API
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want API support for creating, opening, renaming, archiving, and deleting idea spaces so that different research directions are isolated.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide API operations to create, list, get, rename, archive, and delete spaces
Each space stores name, description, status, created_at, and updated_at
Provide API operations to set and read the active space
Deleting or archiving one space does not affect data in other spaces
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Manage idea spaces in the UI
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to manage idea spaces in the local UI so that I do not need to use a CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI lists all idea spaces and their status
The user can create, open, rename, archive, and delete a space
The current active space is clearly visible in the UI
The space description can be edited and saved for later relevance scoring
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Import and store PDF papers
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to import PDFs into the current space so that the system can save papers and prepare them for parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI supports selecting or dragging a PDF into the current active space
The backend saves the original PDF under the current space's papers directory
The system records paper id, file path, file hash, imported_at, and parse status
Duplicate PDFs inside the same space are detected by hash and are not imported twice
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Edit paper metadata
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to view and edit paper metadata so that I can correct failed or incomplete parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: Paper metadata supports title, authors, year, DOI, arXiv ID, PubMed ID, venue, abstract, citation, user tags, and relation to the idea
The UI provides a paper metadata editing form
The user can enter DOI, arXiv, PubMed, or BibTeX text to maintain identifier and citation fields; network lookup is not required in the first version
After saving, the paper list and detail view show the latest metadata
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Parse PDF into passages
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want PDFs parsed into citable passages so that search results can trace back to original text.
acceptanceCriteria: Use PyMuPDF or an equivalent local parser to extract page text from PDFs
Generate passages by page, section cues, and paragraph boundaries
Each passage stores paper id, space id, section, page number, paragraph index, original text, parse confidence, and passage type
If parsing fails, preserve the paper record and error status so the user can retry
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: View papers and passages
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to view paper details and passage text so that I can inspect parsing results.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI shows a paper list for the current space and a paper detail view
The paper detail view shows metadata, parse status, and passages
Each passage shows section, page number, passage type, and original text
The user can navigate from a passage back to its paper
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build full-text search with SQLite FTS5
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want precise full-text search for methods, datasets, metrics, and experimental conditions so that I can find relevant paper passages quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a SQLite FTS5 index for passages
After a paper is parsed, its passages are inserted into or updated in the FTS index
The search API is constrained to the active space or an explicitly provided space
Search results include paper, section, page number, original text snippet, and match score
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Expose literature search in the UI
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to search the current idea space in the UI so that I can manually inspect and locate literature evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI provides a search input for the current space
Search results show paper title, section, page number, snippet, and match hints
A search result can open the corresponding paper and passage
Empty and no-results states are clear
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create and edit knowledge cards
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want structured knowledge cards so that agents can retrieve methods, results, limitations, and evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Knowledge cards support the types Problem, Claim, Evidence, Method, Object, Variable, Metric, Result, Failure Mode, Interpretation, Limitation, and Practical Tip
Each card stores space id, paper id, source passage id, summary, confidence, and user-edited flag
The UI supports viewing, creating, editing, and deleting cards
The user can open a card's source passage
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Extract initial knowledge cards from passages
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want the system to generate initial cards from passages so that manual curation effort is reduced.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide a card extraction job that can be triggered by paper or by space
When no external LLM provider is configured, use a testable local rules-based extractor for method, result, limitation, and metric candidate cards
Every automatically generated card is bound to a source passage and stores confidence and extraction source
Extraction failure does not break full-text search or passage viewing
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Provide structured literature views
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to browse the current space by methods, metrics, results, failure modes, limitations, and claims so that I can understand the literature structure quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI provides views for Methods, Metrics, Results, Failure Modes, Limitations, and Claims
Each view only shows cards from the current active space
Each card shows source paper, page number, and an openable passage link
The user can filter by paper, card type, tag, or relation to the idea
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement core MCP server tools
userStories.description: As an external agent, I want to query the current idea space through a local MCP Server so that I can retrieve literature context.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide a local MCP Server startup entry point
Implement list_spaces, get_active_space, list_papers, search_literature, get_paper_summary, and get_citation tools
All tools default to the active space unless an accessible space id is explicitly provided
search_literature returns source paper, section, page number, passage text, and card information
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement specialized MCP evidence tools
userStories.description: As an external agent, I want task-specific tools for methods, metrics, limitations, failure modes, similar results, and claim evidence so that user questions can be mapped to literature evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement get_methods, get_metrics, get_limitations, find_failure_modes, find_similar_results, compare_with_literature, and get_evidence_for_claim tools
Each tool returns structured results and traceable sources without generating final scientific conclusions
Tool results include paper id, paper title, card id or passage id, page number, and original text snippet
Queries only return content from the current active space
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add agent access controls and reliability checks
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want to know exactly which space an agent can access and ensure retrieval never leaks across projects.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI provides a status control for enabling or disabling local agent access
The UI shows the active space currently exposed to MCP and local connection information
Backend and MCP tests prove that queries never return papers, passages, or cards from other spaces
Every agent-visible result includes source information; source-less results are not returned
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
